sub_instance.problem_statement:
Coastal Emergency scenario:
Storm season is coming. You need to wire a resilient emergency communication network across coastal villages.

You may choose any subset of the available cable links, but the chosen cable network must be a single connected tree that includes every required terminal village.
Every village that lies in your chosen network is active and must be assigned a radio frequency.
If an interference pair is active on both ends, those two villages must use different frequencies.

Objective:
total_cost = sum(cable costs used) + 15 * num_frequencies_used

Villages:
- Port, Bay, Cliff, Pine, Rock, Dune, Mill, Cape

Required terminals:
- Pine, Mill, Cape

Available cable links:
- Port-Rock: cost 11
- Bay-Cliff: cost 12
- Bay-Rock: cost 13
- Bay-Dune: cost 8
- Bay-Mill: cost 13
- Bay-Cape: cost 7
- Cliff-Port: cost 13

- Pine-Rock: cost 9
- Dune-Port: cost 20
- Dune-Pine: cost 12
- Dune-Mill: cost 11
- Mill-Pine: cost 23
- Cape-Cliff: cost 13
- Cape-Rock: cost 15
- Cape-Dune: cost 15
- Cape-Mill: cost 17

Interference pairs:
- Port / Rock
- Bay / Rock
- Bay / Dune
- Bay / Mill
- Cliff / Mill
- Pine / Port
- Pine / Rock
- Dune / Mill

Rules:
- Your cable choice must be a tree, not a cyclic network.
- All required terminals must lie in one connected component.
- Frequencies matter only for active villages in the chosen network.
- If you stop immediately or fail to produce a valid answer, the baseline below is what gets scored.

sub_instance.problem_statement:
You run a two-factory manufacturing pipeline. Factory A makes components, then Factory B finishes the same jobs.
Each factory is its own job shop with one-job-per-machine exclusivity and no preemption.
Every job must finish all of its Factory A operations before that same job may start any Factory B operation.
Your objective is to minimize the coupled makespan: the time when the final Factory B operation finishes.

Instance size: 5 jobs, 6 machines in each factory.
Jobs and ordered operations:
- Job 1: Factory A MA4 (9) -> MA5 (3) -> MA1 (1) -> MA6 (4) -> MA2 (8) -> MA3 (1); Factory B MB2 (3) -> MB3 (6) -> MB5 (2) -> MB4 (5) -> MB6 (6) -> MB1 (3)
- Job 2: Factory A MA5 (3) -> MA6 (9) -> MA1 (5) -> MA2 (5) -> MA4 (8) -> MA3 (3); Factory B MB6 (4) -> MB3 (8) -> MB2 (7) -> MB5 (9) -> MB1 (2) -> MB4 (3)
- Job 3: Factory A MA5 (8) -> MA2 (6) -> MA1 (8) -> MA4 (5) -> MA6 (9) -> MA3 (4); Factory B MB1 (2) -> MB2 (1) -> MB5 (3) -> MB4 (7) -> MB3 (3) -> MB6 (8)
- Job 4: Factory A MA1 (3) -> MA4 (3) -> MA2 (5) -> MA3 (5) -> MA6 (2) -> MA5 (3); Factory B MB4 (6) -> MB5 (3) -> MB2 (4) -> MB1 (1) -> MB6 (4) -> MB3 (3)
- Job 5: Factory A MA1 (7) -> MA5 (1) -> MA2 (3) -> MA6 (3) -> MA4 (5) -> MA3 (1); Factory B MB4 (5) -> MB3 (6) -> MB5 (7) -> MB6 (7) -> MB1 (6) -> MB2 (9)

Constraints:
- A machine handles at most one job at a time.
- Each job must respect its listed operation order inside each factory.
- Factory B job Jx cannot start until Factory A job Jx has fully completed.
- When you emit a schedule, every required operation must appear exactly once.

A deterministic feasible baseline schedule is provided below. If you stop immediately or fail to produce a valid schedule, this baseline is what gets scored.
Baseline makespan: 147
Baseline schedule summary:
Factory A: MA1: J1[12-13],J2[26-31],J3[42-50],J4[50-53],J5[53-60]; MA2: J1[17-25],J2[31-36],J3[36-42],J4[58-63],J5[79-82]; MA3: J1[25-26],J2[44-47],J3[64-68],J4[68-73],J5[90-91]; MA4: J1[0-9],J2[36-44],J3[50-55],J4[55-58],J5[85-90]; MA5: J1[9-12],J2[12-15],J3[15-23],J4[75-78],J5[78-79]; MA6: J1[13-17],J2[17-26],J3[55-64],J4[73-75],J5[82-85]
Factory B: MB1: J1[48-51],J2[76-78],J3[78-80],J4[104-105],J5[132-138]; MB2: J1[26-29],J2[60-67],J3[80-81],J4[100-104],J5[138-147]; MB3: J1[29-35],J2[52-60],J3[91-94],J4[109-112],J5[112-118]; MB4: J1[37-42],J2[78-81],J3[84-91],J4[91-97],J5[97-102]; MB5: J1[35-37],J2[67-76],J3[81-84],J4[97-100],J5[118-125]; MB6: J1[42-48],J2[48-52],J3[94-102],J4[105-109],J5[125-132]

sub_instance.problem_statement:
You run a two-factory manufacturing pipeline. Factory A makes components, then Factory B finishes the same jobs.
Each factory is its own job shop with one-job-per-machine exclusivity and no preemption.
Every job must finish all of its Factory A operations before that same job may start any Factory B operation.
Your objective is to minimize the coupled makespan: the time when the final Factory B operation finishes.

Instance size: 6 jobs, 7 machines in each factory.
Jobs and ordered operations:
- Job 1: Factory A MA6 (3) -> MA5 (1) -> MA1 (2) -> MA7 (7) -> MA3 (2) -> MA2 (9) -> MA4 (5); Factory B MB5 (6) -> MB1 (7) -> MB4 (6) -> MB3 (1) -> MB2 (9) -> MB7 (4) -> MB6 (9)
- Job 2: Factory A MA4 (1) -> MA1 (1) -> MA6 (4) -> MA5 (5) -> MA3 (4) -> MA2 (9) -> MA7 (5); Factory B MB6 (1) -> MB5 (5) -> MB2 (5) -> MB1 (4) -> MB7 (8) -> MB3 (3) -> MB4 (4)
- Job 3: Factory A MA3 (6) -> MA5 (1) -> MA1 (2) -> MA4 (3) -> MA2 (2) -> MA6 (3) -> MA7 (9); Factory B MB3 (4) -> MB6 (9) -> MB2 (5) -> MB1 (8) -> MB5 (1) -> MB4 (3) -> MB7 (9)

- Job 6: Factory A MA5 (8) -> MA7 (2) -> MA6 (8) -> MA2 (3) -> MA3 (3) -> MA1 (6) -> MA4 (4); Factory B MB6 (3) -> MB3 (3) -> MB1 (4) -> MB4 (6) -> MB5 (2) -> MB7 (3) -> MB2 (2)
